verses.text: Genesis begins a new section: the book of the generations of Adam. The opening reminds us — male and female were both made in God's likeness, and God blessed them and called their name Adam, humankind.

Genesis begins a new section: the book of the generations of Adam. The opening reminds us — male and female were both made in God's likeness, and God blessed them and called their name Adam, humankind.
Adam fathered Seth in his own likeness, after his image — notice the echo of how God made Adam. Adam lived 930 years and died. The same word from Eden's curse — surely die — is now quietly fulfilled.
Adam fathered Seth in his own likeness, after his image — notice the echo of how God made Adam. Adam lived 930 years and died. The same word from Eden's curse — surely die — is now quietly fulfilled.
Adam fathered Seth in his own likeness, after his image — notice the echo of how God made Adam. Adam lived 930 years and died. The same word from Eden's curse — surely die — is now quietly fulfilled.
Seth fathered Enos at 105, then lived another 807 years before dying. The line set apart for hope keeps moving forward.
Seth fathered Enos at 105, then lived another 807 years before dying. The line set apart for hope keeps moving forward.
Seth fathered Enos at 105, then lived another 807 years before dying. The line set apart for hope keeps moving forward.
Enos fathered Cainan at 90, then lived another 815 years. Long lives in this chapter — but death still comes for every one of them.
Enos fathered Cainan at 90, then lived another 815 years. Long lives in this chapter — but death still comes for every one of them.
Enos fathered Cainan at 90, then lived another 815 years. Long lives in this chapter — but death still comes for every one of them.
Cainan fathered Mahalaleel at 70, then lived another 840 years. The pattern repeats: birth, more children, death.
Cainan fathered Mahalaleel at 70, then lived another 840 years. The pattern repeats: birth, more children, death.
Cainan fathered Mahalaleel at 70, then lived another 840 years. The pattern repeats: birth, more children, death.
Mahalaleel fathered Jared at 65, then lived another 830 years. The list keeps pressing forward, generation after generation, toward Noah.
Mahalaleel fathered Jared at 65, then lived another 830 years. The list keeps pressing forward, generation after generation, toward Noah.
Mahalaleel fathered Jared at 65, then lived another 830 years. The list keeps pressing forward, generation after generation, toward Noah.
Jared fathered Enoch at 162, then lived another 800 years. The genealogy is about to do something unusual.
Jared fathered Enoch at 162, then lived another 800 years. The genealogy is about to do something unusual.
Jared fathered Enoch at 162, then lived another 800 years. The genealogy is about to do something unusual.

Enoch fathered Methuselah at 65 — then something unique happens. The phrase and he died is replaced with Enoch walked with God; and he was not; for God took him. Only Enoch and later Elijah are taken to God without dying. Hebrews 11 holds him up as a witness of faith.

Enoch fathered Methuselah at 65 — then something unique happens. The phrase and he died is replaced with Enoch walked with God; and he was not; for God took him. Only Enoch and later Elijah are taken to God without dying. Hebrews 11 holds him up as a witness of faith.
Methuselah fathered Lamech at 187, then lived to 969 — the longest life in the Bible. He dies in the year of the flood, just as judgment arrives.
Methuselah fathered Lamech at 187, then lived to 969 — the longest life in the Bible. He dies in the year of the flood, just as judgment arrives.
Methuselah fathered Lamech at 187, then lived to 969 — the longest life in the Bible. He dies in the year of the flood, just as judgment arrives.
Lamech fathers a son and names him Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work. The name Noah sounds like the Hebrew word for comfort. Even in a fading world, hope keeps being named.
Lamech fathers a son and names him Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work. The name Noah sounds like the Hebrew word for comfort. Even in a fading world, hope keeps being named.
Lamech then lived another 595 years and died. The pattern reaches its tenth generation from Adam.
Lamech then lived another 595 years and died. The pattern reaches its tenth generation from Adam.
At 500 years old, Noah fathers Shem, Ham, and Japheth — three names that will repopulate the world after the flood.
